attributes.title: Account Access — Overview
attributes.visState:
{"title": "Account Access — Overview", "type": "markdown", "aggs": [], "params": {"fontSize": 12, "openLinksInNewTab": true, "markdown": "### 🔑 Account Access & Activity
**Who connects to servers (RDP/SSH) and what they do after.** Backed by **`wazuh-alerts-*`** (level ≥3 only — the reliable index, not the archive). Open [Discover](/app/discover) · [Wazuh app](/app/wazuh).

**Logins:** RDP = Windows `4624` logonType `10`; SSH = sshd `Accepted` (rule `5715`); failures = `4625` / PAM `5503` / sshd `5716`. Machine accounts (ending `$`) are excluded from the account tables.

**Actions:** Windows process creation = Sysmon **Event 1** (`user`, full `commandLine`, `logonId`); PowerShell = `4104`; Linux command visibility = **sudo** only (`5402`/`5407`).

🔎 **Drill into one session:** in an RDP logon (`4624`) copy `data.win.eventdata.targetLogonId`, then in the **Session activity** panel below add a filter `data.win.eventdata.logonId : <that value>` **and** `agent.name : <server>` — Sysmon Event 1's `logonId` equals 4624's `targetLogonId`, so you get exactly the processes run in that logon session (scope to the host + the session's time window).

⚠️ Windows **activity** panels (Sysmon Event 1 / PowerShell) only have data once the **level-3 elevation rules** (`access_level3_rules.xml`, incl. Tier 3) are deployed — otherwise they show only already-flagged events. Logins, RDP sessions and Linux activity work as-is."}}

attributes.title: Account Access & Activity (RDP/SSH + actions)
attributes.description: Who connects to servers via RDP/SSH and what they do after (Sysmon Event 1 / PowerShell / sudo). Backed by wazuh-alerts-* (level >=3; deploy the access elevation rules for Windows activity).
